setting.note: An elite Catholic girls' lycee perched high in the mountains of southern Rwanda, close to a spring held to be a source of the Nile. Behind the convent order and the school routine, the Hutu quotas and Tutsi persecution of the wider country play out among the pupils, a rehearsal in miniature for the genocide still to come. The map runs from the forested highlands near the Nile source down to the town of Butare and out to the capital, Kigali.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Scholastique Mukasonga, Notre-Dame du Nil, first published 2012. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: Our Lady of the Nile, Scholastique Mukasonga (2012). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The green of the Nyungwe highland forest that hems the lycee in. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
